riskFloorReasons: safety-invariant proximity: src/monitoring/SessionReaper.ts matches session reaper

causalAutopsy.notes: Latent veto exposed BY #955. The active-process existence-veto has unconditionally kept any session with a live child since it was added (correct for short-lived sessions). #955 removed the open-commitment veto that was the outer blocker; a post-#955-deploy dry-run on echo (v1.3.391) then showed idle sessions moved 100% to keep-reason active-process (their own idle MCP servers), 0 reap-eligible — and the McpProcessReaper correctly refuses to kill MCP procs under a live session, so idle-MCP-heavy sessions sat in a gap neither reaper would touch. This adds the matching staleness bound to the active-process veto and lowers the shared window 24h->8h at the operator's request (so many resource issues; restarts are cheap). Not a regression — #955 peeled the outer layer and revealed this inner one.
